classifications.phrase: Inflation continued to ease in the December quarter
inflation remains high at 4.1 per cent
continuing excess demand in the economy
Higher interest rates are working to establish a more sustainable balance
conditions in the labour market continue to ease gradually
they remain tighter than is consistent with sustained full employment
the Board remains highly attentive to inflation risks
a further increase in interest rates cannot be ruled out
Returning inflation to target within a reasonable timeframe remains the Boards highest priority

payload.rationale: The RBA signals a hawkish stance on inflation control, emphasizing persistent inflation risks and explicitly noting that further rate increases cannot be ruled out, while acknowledging some easing in goods prices and gradual labour market softening.
